regimens.setting: Unresectable or metastatic tumors that have progressed following prior treatment

drugs.name: Pembrolizumab
drugs.dose: 200 mg IV Day 1, repeat every 3 weeks or 400 mg IV Day 1, repeat every 6 weeks

biomarkers.requirement: Tumor must be MSI-high or mismatch repair deficient

trial_data.n_patients: 233 patients with advanced MSI-H/dMMR noncolorectal cancers
trial_data.trial_statistics: ORR 30.8%, median duration of response 47.5 months

regimens.notes: Pembrolizumab is a systemic treatment option for adult and pediatric patients with unresectable or metastatic, MSI-H or dMMR solid tumors that have progressed following prior treatment and who have no satisfactory alternative treatment options [1]. Not for giant cell tumor of bone. Pembrolizumab and berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV pembrolizumab [2].

regimens.setting: Unresectable or metastatic tumors that have progressed following prior treatment

trial_data.n_patients: Patients with advanced or metastatic solid TMB-H tumors
trial_data.trial_statistics: ORR 35.3% for tTMB-H in interim analysis, 22.5% for bTMB-H in final analysis

regimens.notes: Based on data from a randomized, open-label, phase II study in patients with advanced or metastatic solid TMB-H tumors [4].

trial_data.n_patients: 33 patients with grade 1 or 2 chondrosarcoma
trial_data.trial_statistics: 6-month PFS 47%, four patients (12%) had stable disease for >1 year

regimens.notes: Dasatinib is a small-molecule inhibitor of kinases including SRC family, BCR-ABL, c-KIT, and PDGFRs. In a phase 2 study, 6 patients had objective tumor response and four patients had stable disease for >1 year, suggesting some tumor control [5,6].

trial_data.n_patients: 42 patients with unresectable or metastatic CCS
trial_data.trial_statistics: DCR 43% at 16 weeks, median PFS 7.9 months, median OS 17.6 months

regimens.notes: Pazopanib is an oral, multikinase inhibitor with antiangiogenic activity. A single-arm, multicenter, phase 2 study evaluated its safety and efficacy in patients with unresectable or metastatic conventional chondrosarcoma [7].

trial_data.n_patients: 21 patients with advanced chondrosarcoma
trial_data.trial_statistics: Median PFS 5.6 months, PFS at 6 months 39.5%, 52% had stable disease

regimens.notes: Ivosidenib is a selective IDH1 inhibitor. Testing for IDH1 mutation can be performed by NGS or targeted exon sequencing [8].
For dedifferentiated chondrosarcoma. Data supporting these agents are from phase 2 studies in advanced sarcomas [7,9].

trial_data.n_patients: 5 patients with chondrosarcoma (pembrolizumab), 28 patients with relapsed sarcomas (nivolumab)
trial_data.trial_statistics: 1 partial response in 5 patients with chondrosarcoma (pembrolizumab); 1 partial response in 1 patient with dedifferentiated chondrosarcoma (nivolumab)

regimens.notes: Efficacy data supporting PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitors as monotherapy in these tumors are limited. Based on data from SARC028 and a retrospective review [10,11]. Pembrolizumab and berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV pembrolizumab [2]. Nivolumab and hyaluronidase-nvhy subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV nivolumab [12].

trial_data.n_patients: 38 evaluable patients with advanced bone sarcomas, 14 with chondrosarcoma (10 CCS, 4 DDCS)
trial_data.trial_statistics: Median PFS 9.5 months for CCS, 1.8 months for DDCS; Median OS 11.9 months, ORR 5%

regimens.notes: Combination therapy evaluated in adult patients with advanced bone sarcomas. One individual with DDCS had a complete response (>4 years) [13]. Nivolumab and hyaluronidase-nvhy subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV nivolumab [12].

trial_data.n_patients: 56 patients with advanced chordoma
trial_data.trial_statistics: CBR 64%, median PFS 9 months, 70% had stable disease

regimens.notes: Imatinib is a TKI. Phase II trial data showed a CBR of 64% and median PFS of 9 months in the intention-to-treat population [14].

trial_data.n_patients: 32 patients with unresectable, recurrent, or metastatic chordoma
trial_data.trial_statistics: 6-month PFS 54%, median PFS 6.3 months, 5-year OS 18%

regimens.notes: Dasatinib is a small-molecule inhibitor of kinases. No substantial differences in ORR, median PFS, or 6-month PFS compared with imatinib or lapatinib treatment [5,6].

regimens.setting: Advanced, refractory or resistant to standard therapy

trial_data.n_patients: 34 patients with chordoma
trial_data.trial_statistics: Objective response in 4 individuals at week 12, median PFS 6.1 months (95% CI, 4.6–11.5)

regimens.notes: Based on a non-randomized, open-label, phase II basket trial in patients with rare and ultrarare sarcomas [15]. Pembrolizumab and berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV pembrolizumab [2].

trial_data.n_patients: 9 patients with advanced chordoma
trial_data.trial_statistics: 44% (4/9) achieved stable disease for at least 16 weeks

regimens.notes: Based on a phase II study in patients with advanced chordoma [16].

regimens.setting: Advanced disease resistant to prior imatinib
regimens.notes: Imatinib in combination with cisplatin has been effective in a small series of patients with advanced chordoma resistant to prior imatinib therapy [17].

regimens.setting: Advanced disease resistant to prior imatinib
regimens.notes: Imatinib in combination with sirolimus has been effective in a small series of patients with advanced chordoma resistant to prior imatinib therapy [18].

trial_data.n_patients: 1 patient with treatment refractory chordoma
trial_data.trial_statistics: Response to erlotinib reported

regimens.notes: Efficacy of EGFR inhibitors such as erlotinib has been demonstrated in patients with advanced chordoma resistant to imatinib [19].

trial_data.n_patients: 18 patients with locally advanced and metastatic chordoma
trial_data.trial_statistics: Partial response in 33%, stable disease in 39% (Choi criteria); median PFS 6 months (Choi), 8 months (RECIST)

regimens.notes: Phase II study on lapatinib in advanced EGFR-positive chordoma [20].

trial_data.n_patients: 27 patients with advanced/metastatic chordoma
trial_data.trial_statistics: Objective response 1/27 (3.7%), 9-month PFS 73.0%, 12-month OS 86.5%

regimens.notes: Based on data from a phase II trial in patients with advanced/metastatic chordoma [21,22].

regimens.setting: First-line therapy (primary/neoadjuvant/adjuvant therapy) for localized disease

trial_data.trial_name: POG-CCG INT-0091, Children's Oncology Group study
trial_data.n_patients: 398 patients with non-metastatic Ewing sarcoma (INT-0091); 568 patients with localized Ewing sarcoma (Children's Oncology Group study)
trial_data.trial_statistics: 5-year EFS 69% vs 54% for VACD-IE vs VACD alone (INT-0091); 5-year EFS 73% vs 65% for every-2-week vs every-3-week schedule (Children's Oncology Group study)

regimens.notes: VDC-IE is the preferred regimen for localized disease and is a category 1 recommendation. In patients <18 years, evidence supports 2-week compressed treatment [23,24]. Dactinomycin can be substituted for doxorubicin for concerns regarding cardiotoxicity [25].

trial_data.trial_statistics: 3-year EFS rates were 73% and 74%, respectively, for VACD and VAID (EICESS-92)

regimens.notes: VAIA is another recommended regimen for Ewing sarcoma. The EICESS-92 study investigated whether cyclophosphamide has efficacy similar to ifosfamide [26,27].

trial_data.n_patients: 640 patients between ages 2 and 49 years
trial_data.trial_statistics: EFS at 3 years 61% for VIDE vs 67% for VDC-IE

regimens.notes: VIDE is a European induction regimen. The EURO EWING 2012 trial compared VIDE induction followed by various consolidation regimens vs compressed VDC-IE induction [28].

regimens.setting: Primary therapy for metastatic disease at initial presentation
regimens.notes: For metastatic Ewing sarcoma, VDC is one of the preferred regimens for primary therapy at initial presentation.

regimens.setting: Primary therapy for metastatic disease at initial presentation

trial_data.n_patients: 120 patients with metastatic disease
trial_data.trial_statistics: 5-year EFS rate was 22% for both VACD-IE and VACD groups; 5-year OS rate was 34% and 35%, respectively

regimens.notes: VDC-IE is a preferred regimen for metastatic Ewing sarcoma at initial presentation. The INT-0091 study showed no significant difference in EFS and OS between VACD-IE and VACD regimens in patients with metastatic disease [23].

regimens.setting: Primary therapy for metastatic disease at initial presentation

trial_data.n_patients: 640 patients between ages 2 and 49 years
trial_data.trial_statistics: EFS at 3 years 61% for VIDE vs 67% for VDC-IE

regimens.notes: VIDE is a preferred induction regimen for metastatic Ewing sarcoma. The EURO EWING 2012 trial compared VIDE induction followed by various consolidation regimens vs compressed VDC-IE induction [28].

trial_data.n_patients: 54 patients with relapsed or refractory Ewing sarcoma
trial_data.trial_statistics: Responses in 44% of patients (35% CR, 9% PR), 26% in continuous remission after median follow-up of 23 months

regimens.notes: Vincristine could be added to this regimen. Cyclophosphamide and topotecan have been associated with favorable response rates in relapsed or refractory bone sarcomas [29,30].

trial_data.n_patients: 20 evaluable patients with recurrent or progressive Ewing sarcoma
trial_data.trial_statistics: Overall objective response rate 63%, median time to progression 8.3 months (16.2 months for subset with recurrent disease)

regimens.notes: Vincristine could be added to this regimen. Irinotecan and temozolomide have been associated with favorable response rates in relapsed or refractory bone sarcomas [31,32,33,34].

trial_data.trial_statistics: ORR 68%

regimens.notes: Combination therapy with vincristine, irinotecan, and temozolomide appears active and well-tolerated in patients with relapsed or refractory Ewing sarcoma [35].

trial_data.n_patients: 39 patients with Ewing sarcoma
trial_data.trial_statistics: 6-month objective response 26%, median OS 10.2 months, median PFS 4.4 months, 42% metabolic tumor response

regimens.notes: Cabozantinib is a VEGFR2 TKI with inhibitory activity against the MET receptor. Based on data from the CABONE trial [36].

trial_data.n_patients: 22 children and young adults with refractory bone sarcomas
trial_data.trial_statistics: Median duration of response 4.8 months, objective response rate 29%

regimens.notes: Vincristine could be added to this regimen. Based on a retrospective study evaluating docetaxel in combination with gemcitabine in children and young adults with refractory bone sarcomas [37].

trial_data.n_patients: 73 patients treated with IFOS vs 73 with TC
trial_data.trial_statistics: Median EFS 5.7 months for IFOS vs 3.7 months for TC; median OS 16.8 months for IFOS vs 10.4 months for TC

regimens.notes: Vincristine could be added to this regimen. The phase III rEECur trial compared high-dose ifosfamide against topotecan and cyclophosphamide [38].

trial_data.n_patients: 30 patients with metastatic Ewing sarcoma
trial_data.trial_statistics: 8-week progression-free rate 63%, median OS 53 weeks

regimens.notes: Based on data from the phase II SARC024 trial in patients with metastatic Ewing sarcoma whose disease progressed following at least 1 line of therapy [39].

trial_data.n_patients: 97 patients with relapsed or refractory bone sarcomas
trial_data.trial_statistics: ORR 51%

regimens.notes: Vincristine could be added to this regimen. Based on three Children's Cancer Group phase I/II trials [40].

trial_data.n_patients: 28 adult patients with relapsed Ewing sarcoma
trial_data.trial_statistics: ORR 14.3%, median duration of response 4.2 months

regimens.notes: Myelosuppression was reversible and primary G-CSF prophylaxis may be considered during lurbinectedin treatment (category 2B) [41].

regimens.setting: Resectable with unacceptable morbidity and/or unresectable axial lesions

trial_data.n_patients: 37 patients with unresectable or recurrent GCTB
trial_data.trial_statistics: Tumor response in 86% (30 of 35 evaluable patients)

regimens.notes: Denosumab is a fully humanized monoclonal antibody against the RANK ligand. Consider consultation with dentist prior to initial therapy. May be continued until disease progression in responding disease [42,43].

regimens.setting: First-line therapy (primary/neoadjuvant/adjuvant therapy or metastatic disease)

trial_data.trial_statistics: 3-year and 5-year OS rates were 65% and 55%, respectively, in both groups

regimens.notes: Cisplatin and doxorubicin is a category 1 recommended regimen for first-line therapy. The combination was better tolerated compared to a multi-drug regimen with no difference in survival [44].

regimens.setting: First-line therapy (primary/neoadjuvant/adjuvant therapy or metastatic disease)

trial_data.trial_statistics: 6-year EFS 63% and OS 74% for 3-drug regimen vs 64% and 70% for 4-drug regimen

regimens.notes: MAP is a category 1 recommended regimen for first-line therapy. Preferred in patients <40 years with excellent performance status. In the event a patient receiving high-dose methotrexate experiences delayed elimination due to renal impairment, glucarpidase is strongly recommended [45,46].

regimens.setting: First-line therapy (primary/neoadjuvant/adjuvant therapy or metastatic disease)
regimens.notes: An intensified 4-drug regimen. In the event a patient receiving high-dose methotrexate experiences delayed elimination due to renal impairment, glucarpidase is strongly recommended [47].

trial_data.trial_statistics: Response rate 48% in patients with relapsed or refractory osteosarcoma

regimens.notes: Based on data from a phase II trial by the French Society of Pediatric Oncology [48].

trial_data.n_patients: 30 patients with relapsed and unresectable high-grade osteosarcoma
trial_data.trial_statistics: PFS at 4 months 46%, median PFS 4 months, median OS 7 months, CBR 29%

regimens.notes: Based on a phase II trial by the Italian Sarcoma Group in patients with relapsed and unresectable high-grade osteosarcoma after failure of standard multimodal therapy [51].
Single-agent gemcitabine has shown activity in relapsed or refractory bone sarcomas [52].

trial_data.n_patients: 38 patients with unresectable or relapsed high-grade osteosarcoma

regimens.notes: This regimen was active in the second-line setting, but toxicity required dose reductions and/or treatment interruptions in 66% of patients [53].

trial_data.trial_statistics: 19% response rate, 35% stable disease, PFS at 4 months 42%

regimens.notes: Cyclophosphamide and etoposide combination therapy for relapsed high-risk osteosarcoma [54].

trial_data.trial_statistics: Higher histologic response rate for ifosfamide/etoposide regimen vs high-dose methotrexate/doxorubicin regimen (56% vs 39%), but 5-year OS was similar

regimens.notes: In the event a patient receiving high-dose methotrexate experiences delayed elimination due to renal impairment, glucarpidase is strongly recommended [55].
In the event a patient receiving high-dose methotrexate experiences delayed elimination due to renal impairment, glucarpidase is strongly recommended.

regimens.setting: Relapsed or refractory disease beyond second-line therapy

trial_data.n_patients: 18 patients with recurrent or metastatic osteosarcoma
trial_data.trial_statistics: Metabolic response in 1 patient, mixed responses in 4 patients, median OS time 25 weeks

regimens.notes: Radium 223 dichloride is an alpha-emitting, bone-targeting radiopharmaceutical [56].

regimens.setting: Unresectable or metastatic tumors that have progressed following prior treatment

drugs.name: Pembrolizumab
drugs.dose: 200 mg IV Day 1, repeat every 3 weeks or 400 mg IV Day 1, repeat every 6 weeks

trial_data.n_patients: Patients with advanced solid tumors
trial_data.trial_statistics: ORR 29% in tTMB-H group vs 6% in non-tTMB-H group

regimens.notes: TMB testing is recommended for patients with unresectable or metastatic tumors who have progressed following prior treatment and who have no satisfactory alternative treatment options [3]. Not for giant cell tumor of bone. Pembrolizumab and berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV pembrolizumab [2]. Nivolumab and hyaluronidase-nvhy subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV nivolumab [12].
Sunitinib is a multikinase inhibitor. Included as a category 2A recommendation for metastatic and widespread chondrosarcoma [9].
For dedifferentiated chondrosarcoma. Nivolumab and hyaluronidase-nvhy subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV nivolumab [12].
